commands.review_summary:
The PR updates waited A2A reply extraction to ignore OpenClaw/message-tool transcript mirror rows, preserves wait metadata, canonicalizes same-session requester keys, and adds focused run-wait, sessions_send, and Gateway loopback regressions.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +460. Total +499 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reads the latest assistant text without filtering OpenClaw delivery/message-tool mirror rows, and the PR adds focused regressions for that path. I did not establish a live Gateway or transport reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review and contributor real behavior proof are needed before merge; there is no narrow code repair finding for automation to fix.

target.title: fix: A2A handoffs can duplicate message-tool replies
target.body:
Related: #39476
Related: #91161
Related: #93293

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where A2A `sessions_send` could treat an already-delivered `message(action="send")` source reply as the target agent's newest assistant reply. When the durable transcript mirror trailed session history, the waited A2A path could feed that mirror into ping-pong or announce delivery and produce duplicate visible output.

This is a narrow companion to #39476. It does not replace #91161's reverse `sessions_send` tool guard, and it does not change compaction replay behavior covered by #93293.

## What Changed

- `waitForAgentRunAndReadUpdatedAssistantReply` now preserves the normalized `agent.wait` metadata while adding `replyText`.
- Waited reply extraction ignores transcript-only OpenClaw assistant artifacts: raw delivery mirrors, projected message-tool mirrors, and gateway-injected rows.
- Same-session A2A routing now uses the canonical requester key internally, so aliases such as `main` do not accidentally take the cross-session path.
- Added a Gateway loopback regression that uses the real in-process Gateway, `agent.wait`, `chat.history`, `runSessionsSendA2AFlow`, and a fake outbound WhatsApp adapter to prove that a trailing message-tool delivery mirror is not re-announced.

No config, secret handling, channel adapter contract, tool schema, or delivery API is changed.

## User Impact

A2A handoffs no longer turn an already-delivered message-tool source reply into a second visible reply. Normal model-authored assistant replies still flow through the existing waited reply, ping-pong, and announce behavior.

## Evidence

Source-level red/green proof from the existing focused regressions:

```text
# RED proof with only the transcript-artifact guard temporarily disabled:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/run-wait.test.ts

Test Files  1 failed (1)
Tests  4 failed | 27 passed (31)

Representative old behavior:
expected replyText undefined, received "already delivered source reply"
expected undefined, received "gateway notice"
```

Gateway loopback proof without an external proof service or live WhatsApp send:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts -t "does not re-announce a trailing message-tool delivery mirror after a waited A2A run"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 4 skipped (5)
```

The loopback test seeds a real session transcript with a `message` tool delivery mirror, verifies `chat.history` projects that mirror, completes `agent.wait`, runs `runSessionsSendA2AFlow`, and asserts the fake WhatsApp outbound `sendText` call list stays empty.

Focused validation after the final patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts src/agents/run-wait.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts

Test Files  2 passed (2) in gateway shard
Tests  10 passed (10)

Test Files  3 passed (3) in agents shard
Tests  90 passed (90)
```

Additional local checks:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core:test
# passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/run-wait.ts src/agents/run-wait.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts
# passed

pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/run-wait.ts src/agents/run-wait.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts
# passed

git diff --check
# passed
```

Clawpatch:

```text
npx -y clawpatch@latest map --source auto
npx -y clawpatch@latest review --feature feat_library_8ceadac811 --jobs 1
npx -y clawpatch@latest review --feature feat_library_43759f9b06 --jobs 1

npx -y clawpatch@latest revalidate --finding fnd_sig-feat-library-8ceadac811-862d_ef45fcedf5
# fixed: updated reply helper dropped successful wait metadata

npx -y clawpatch@latest revalidate --finding fnd_sig-feat-library-43759f9b06-1138_4d6759e636
# fixed: A2A same-session routing used the raw requester key after canonicalization
```

Clawpatch still reports unrelated open findings in untouched files (`sessions-spawn-tool.ts`, `run-session-target.ts`, and `sessions-list-tool.ts`). They are not part of this duplicate-delivery patch.

Local check notes:

- No live production WhatsApp message was sent. The added proof uses the repo's Gateway test harness and mocks only the outbound WhatsApp adapter boundary.
- Global `pnpm format:check` currently fails on existing unrelated files across the repo; targeted format for the touched files passes.
- Broad `pnpm lint:core` hit a TypeScript-Go parser crash while scanning unrelated dependency typings; targeted oxlint for the touched files passes.

AI-assisted: yes. Prepared with Codex; reviewed and revalidated with Clawpatch. I understand the code change.
